tossups.question: A composer from this country performed many of her seven violin concertos before being injured in a car accident. Pieces like Venetian Games exemplify the "limited aleatoricism" of a composer from this non-Hungary country who used steady F-sharps on the timpani in 9/8 time to begin his Concerto for Orchestra. A choral work from this country based on the B-A-C-H motif is called the St. Luke Passion. A composer from this country set a message written on the wall of a Gestapo cell in his Symphony of Sorrowful Songs. A tone cluster-filled piece from this country for 52 string players is titled Threnody for the Victims of Hiroshima. For 10 points, name this home country of Witold Lutosławski ("VEE-told loo-toh-SWAFF-skee"), Henryk Gorecki ("HEN-rick goo-RET-skee"), and Krzysztof Penderecki ("k’SHISH-toff pen-duh-RET-skee").
tossups.question_sanitized: A composer from this country performed many of her seven violin concertos before being injured in a car accident. Pieces like Venetian Games exemplify the "limited aleatoricism" of a composer from this non-Hungary country who used steady F-sharps on the timpani in 9/8 time to begin his Concerto for Orchestra. A choral work from this country based on the B-A-C-H motif is called the St. Luke Passion. A composer from this country set a message written on the wall of a Gestapo cell in his Symphony of Sorrowful Songs. A tone cluster-filled piece from this country for 52 string players is titled Threnody for the Victims of Hiroshima. For 10 points, name this home country of Witold Lutoslawski ("VEE-told loo-toh-SWAFF-skee"), Henryk Gorecki ("HEN-rick goo-RET-skee"), and Krzysztof Penderecki ("k'SHISH-toff pen-duh-RET-skee").
tossups.answer: Poland [Republic of Poland; or Rzeczpospolita Polska] [The composer in the first clue is Grażyna Bacewicz.]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Poland [Republic of Poland; or Rzeczpospolita Polska] [The composer in the first clue is Grazyna Bacewicz.]

bonuses.leadin: Krzysztof Penderecki (“k’SHISH-toff pen-deh-RET-skee”) wrote for 48-piece and 52-piece ensembles of these instruments in his pieces Polymorphia and Threnody to the Victims of Hiroshima. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Krzysztof Penderecki ("k'SHISH-toff pen-deh-RET-skee") wrote for 48-piece and 52-piece ensembles of these instruments in his pieces Polymorphia and Threnody to the Victims of Hiroshima. For 10 points each:
parts: Samuel Barber adapted the slow movement of a B-flat minor chamber piece into an Adagio for what section of the orchestra?

The third movement of this composer’s 1948 Concerto for String Orchestra, her most performed piece, is characteristically marked Vivo. This composer and violinist was nicknamed “The First Lady of Polish Music.”

Critics applied this stylistic label to most of Bacewicz’s music, although she rejected it. Igor Stravinsky’s works in this style include a Concerto in D for string orchestra, his violin concerto, and the Symphony of Psalms.
parts_sanitized: Samuel Barber adapted the slow movement of a B-flat minor chamber piece into an Adagio for what section of the orchestra?

The third movement of this composer's 1948 Concerto for String Orchestra, her most performed piece, is characteristically marked Vivo. This composer and violinist was nicknamed "The First Lady of Polish Music."

Critics applied this stylistic label to most of Bacewicz's music, although she rejected it. Igor Stravinsky's works in this style include a Concerto in D for string orchestra, his violin concerto, and the Symphony of Psalms.
answers_sanitized: strings [or string instruments; accept string orchestra; accept Adagio for Strings]
Grazyna Bacewicz ("grah-ZHEE-nah bat-SEV-eech") [or Grazyna Bacewicz Biernacka]

packet.name: D - UBC B, Oxford A, Waterloo B, CWRU A, Columbia C
